[cron:14f1ebf8-f4d4-478c-bfad-f1f5c9af7925 Mindset Labs Vacation Daily Report] Using the research synthesis provided earlier today (Purpose, Productivity, Confidence, Financial Discipline, Messaging), continue improving the Mindset Shift Labs autonomous assessment system. Each run: (1) make one concrete improvement to scoring/questions/routing/copy, (2) sanity-check for ethical and plain-language compliance, (3) produce a concise Daily Vacation Report with what changed, why it matters, current risks, and next action. Keep report executive-brief length.
Current time: Saturday, March 21st, 2026 — 18:55 (America/Chicago) / 2026-03-21 23:55 UTC

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.

content.text:
## D8 Daily Learning Checkpoint (18:53 CT)
- Trigger: scheduled reminder for D8 90-day campaign daily learning checkpoint.
- Completed internal review of all 13 D8 activation files, latest war-room packet, and latest scheduler telemetry.
- Findings: 13/13 agent files still missing same-day individualized learning/blocker/risk updates; GHL auth blocker persists (401 invalid/expired token); content queue remains empty with 7/7 day gap.
- Output written: `reports/2026-03-21-d8-learning-checkpoint-1853-ct.md`.

content.name: write

arguments.content:
# Mindset Shift Labs — Daily Vacation Report (2026-03-21 18:55 CT)

## What changed (one concrete improvement)
Added a **productivity consistency guardrail** to `skills/assessment-handler.mjs`:
- If `action_consistency <= 3/10` and computed score > 80, score is now capped at 80.
- This prevents low-execution leads from being routed into top-tier/high-ticket pathways too early.
- Also added reason-specific tagging (`guardrail-productivity-consistency`) and plain-language alert copy (`daily follow-through too low for high-ticket routing`).

## Why it matters
This aligns routing with real readiness (Purpose + Productivity synthesis): strong intent without daily follow-through should receive capacity-building nurture first, improving fit, trust, and close quality.

## Ethical + plain-language sanity check
- Ethical: reduces over-promising/misrouting risk; avoids pressure-selling people not yet execution-ready.
- Plain language: replaced internal jargon in alert text with simple wording (“daily follow-through”).

## Current risks
- Single-question proxy risk: productivity currently inferred from one field (`action_consistency`) and may miss nuance.
- Guardrail order effect: if multiple weak signals exist, only first matched reason is recorded.

## Next action
Add a lightweight **confidence guardrail** next run (cap high-ticket routing when self-efficacy signal is very low), then update result-page copy to explain next-step support in non-judgmental language.

content.text:
Mindset Shift Labs — Daily Vacation Report (Exec Brief)

What changed:
- Implemented one concrete improvement in `skills/assessment-handler.mjs`: a new **productivity consistency guardrail**.
- If `action_consistency <= 3/10` and score is above 80, routing score is now capped at 80 (prevents premature high-ticket routing).
- Added reason-specific tagging and clearer copy:
  - New tag: `guardrail-productivity-consistency`
  - Plain-language alert text: “daily follow-through too low for high-ticket routing.”

Why it matters:
- Improves readiness-based routing (not just intent-based scoring).
- Reduces mismatch between lead stage and offer intensity, protecting trust and conversion quality.

Ethical + plain-language sanity check:
- Ethical: lowers risk of over-escalation/pressure-selling to low-readiness leads.
- Plain language: replaced jargon with clearer user-facing phrasing.

Current risks:
- Productivity is still inferred from a single signal (`action_consistency`), which may oversimplify readiness.
- Guardrail logic records only the first triggered reason when multiple weak signals coexist.

Next action:
- Add a confidence-based guardrail next run, then align results-page copy so “why this recommendation” is clear, supportive, and non-judgmental.
